title: Could Opendoor Stock Hit $10 in 2026?
text: The article examines whether Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) can return to $10 per share in 2026 after its dramatic 2025 rally. What Drove the 2025 Rally: The stock surged from under $1 to $10.87 primarily due to meme mania fueled by investor Eric Jackson's bullish call and extremely high $82 price target. This sparked retail investor enthusiasm reminiscent of the 2021 meme stock era. Additional catalysts included the return of co-founders to the board and a new CEO from Shopify, plus optimism about AI-driven cost reduction. Why History May Not Repeat: The author identifies several headwinds: Housing Market Stalled: Despite expectations for recovery, the housing market remains frozen as existing homeowners remain locked into homes at low interest rates while rates stay elevated. Profitability Remains Elusive: For 2026, analysts project only modest improvements, with losses narrowing from -$0.25 to approximately -$0.15 per share. Recommendation: The author advises waiting for substantive positive developments.
